texto: That which we most desire, we worship as our god; for that which is chiefly desired is the chief good in his account, who so desires it. And what he counts his chief good, that he makes his god. Desire is an act of worship . . . and to be most desired is that worship, that honor, which is due only to God. To desire anything more or so much as the enjoyment of God is to idolize it, to prostrate the heart to it, and worship it as God only should be worshipped. He only should be that one thing desirable to us above all things. . . .
explicacao: Orientação direta de David Clarkson sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “That which we most desire, we worship as our god; for that which is chiefly desired is the chief …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, worship, which, útil em momentos de reflexao.
